title: Online Banking Brute-Force Login
scenario: Credential attack against retail banking customers
alert: SIEM Alert ID BNK-LOGIN-2026-001 | Online banking portal detected 1,247 failed logins in 18 minutes against 43 customer accounts. Source IPs include 185.199.108.44 and 91.240.118.172. Several attempts used usernames from a previous credential leak. Five accounts were locked after multiple failed login attempts. User agent: python-requests/2.31. Possible brute-force or password spraying campaign targeting retail banking customers.

title: Phishing Email Targeting Bank Employees
alert: Email Gateway Alert BNK-MAIL-2026-014 | 38 employees in treasury and customer support received an urgent message from payroll-update@secure-bank-benefits.com. Subject: Urgent password reset required for Q2 payroll. Body asks users to verify your account at https://bank-portal-secure-login.example.com/auth. Attachment name: Benefits_Update.html. Sender domain was registered recently and fails DMARC alignment. Possible phishing campaign targeting bank employee credentials.

title: SQL Injection Attempt on Banking Portal
alert: WAF Alert BNK-WAF-2026-077 | Public online banking portal /accounts/search received suspicious request from 203.0.113.88. Payload: accountId=105 OR '1'='1' UNION SELECT card_number, cvv, expiry_date FROM cards--. WAF blocked the request and triggered SQL injection signature. Target asset: web-banking-prod-01. Customer card data exposure risk if exploit succeeds.

title: Suspicious ATM Withdrawals
alert: ATM Fraud Alert BNK-ATM-2026-032 | Customer account CUST-884201 had 11 cash withdrawals from ATMs in three cities within 42 minutes. Locations: Casablanca, Rabat, and Marrakech. Total withdrawal amount: 48,500 MAD. Card-present transactions occurred shortly after failed PIN attempts at ATM-RA-112. Pattern differs from customer baseline and may indicate card cloning or account takeover.

title: Abnormal Outbound Traffic and Data Exfiltration
scenario: Possible exfiltration of sensitive bank data
alert: Firewall Alert BNK-FW-2026-209 | Core banking reporting server FIN-REPORT-02 uploaded 3.8 GB to external host 198.51.100.77 over HTTPS between 02:10 and 02:42. Destination domain: files-sync-cloud.example.net. The server normally sends less than 50 MB outbound per day. DLP detected filenames matching customer_statement_export_*.csv. Possible data exfiltration from internal banking network.

title: Malware Alert on Employee Workstation
alert: EDR Alert BNK-EDR-2026-116 | Employee workstation WS-CREDIT-044 executed suspicious PowerShell command that downloaded payload from http://updates-office365.example.org/a.ps1. EDR quarantined file with SHA256 hash 2d711642b726b04401627ca9fbac32f5c8530fb1903cc4db02258717921a4881. Process attempted credential dumping behavior and connection to 45.77.12.89. User belongs to credit operations department.

title: Impossible Travel Login
scenario: Possible account takeover of bank employee account
alert: IAM Alert BNK-IAM-2026-041 | Employee user treasury.ops@bank.example successfully authenticated from Casablanca at 09:12 and then from Singapore IP 103.21.244.18 at 09:47. MFA push was approved on the second login after three denied prompts. User account has access to treasury reporting dashboards. Possible impossible travel, MFA fatigue, or compromised credentials.

title: Suspicious SWIFT Transfer Approval
scenario: Potential fraudulent wire transfer or business email compromise
alert: Payment Alert BNK-SWIFT-2026-018 | SWIFT transfer approval requested for 2,750,000 EUR to new beneficiary account created 19 minutes earlier. Approval came from finance.approver@bank.example outside normal working hours. Source IP 196.12.44.21 differs from the user's normal country. Email thread referenced urgent wire transfer and executive request. Possible BEC-driven fraudulent transfer.

title: Ransomware Behavior On File Server
source: Endpoint detection and file integrity monitoring
scenario: Possible ransomware spreading inside banking network
alert: EDR Alert BNK-FILE-2026-063 | File server FS-BRANCH-07 renamed 8,932 files in 11 minutes and created ransom_note.txt in multiple customer document folders. Process tree shows powershell.exe launching unknown binary update_service.exe. Hash observed: 9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6. SMB write activity originated from workstation WS-LOAN-091. Possible ransomware encryption activity.

title: Privileged Account Misuse
scenario: Potential insider threat or administrator account compromise
alert: PAM Alert BNK-ADMIN-2026-025 | Admin account core-db-admin performed bulk export from customer_accounts and transaction_history tables at 01:33. Session originated from 10.40.8.22, outside the normal jump server path. Command history includes SELECT * FROM customer_accounts and compressed archive customer_data_export.zip. No approved change ticket found. Possible privileged account misuse or insider data theft.
